problem.description: Existing combat.property.test.ts doesn't match current API, references non-existent LootService
options: Update all tests now (including unimplemented features)
Implement LootService first, then update tests
Implement available properties now (5 & 7), defer loot-dependent tests (6 & 8)

agentResponses.rationale: Property tests should match existing implementation. Don't write tests for non-existent features. Properties 5 & 7 are immediately testable with current API. Properties 6 & 8 require LootService - defer until implementation.

Property-based tests provide high value for combat logic. Test what exists now, expand coverage as features are added. Properties 5 (damage consistency) and 7 (character death) are critical. Can add loot properties later without affecting existing test quality.
Updating tests for non-existent features wastes time. Current API supports Properties 5 & 7 fully. Estimated 2-3 hours for Properties 5 & 7 (vs 4-5 for all). Clean separation allows future expansion.

finalDecision.approach: Phased implementation - Properties 5 & 7 now, Properties 6 & 8 after LootService
finalDecision.rationale: Test existing implementation first, expand coverage as features are added
implementation: Property 5: Damage Calculation Consistency (damage formula, critical hits, defense)
Property 7: Character Death (death detection, health boundaries)

outcome.actualResult: Properties 5 & 7 implemented with 11 tests, 100% pass rate, 1100+ test cases executed
